company.what_they_do: SGA Youth & Family Services is a Chicago-based nonprofit human services agency (founded 1911) that provides free, comprehensive social services to children, families, and communities in Chicagoland's most underserved neighborhoods. Their work is organized around a proprietary framework called the 'Cycle of Opportunity®,' which targets four core service areas: Parenting, Early Childhood Education, Education Supports, and Workforce Development. Services span from pregnancy through career ('cradle to career') and are delivered across Cook County, Lake County, and Will County, Illinois. Specific programs include Early Head Start and Head Start, parenting education, mentoring and restorative justice for teens (ages 14–18), community services for stability and well-being, and subsidized youth employment for ages 16–24. The mission is to disrupt cycles of poverty and adversity and replace them with lasting community change.
raw_notes: Founded 1911; one of Chicago's oldest human service agencies.

Originally inspired by Hull House reformers; later merged with Scholarship Association for Jewish Children to become 'Scholarship and Guidance Association (SGA)'; subsequently renamed SGA Youth & Family Services to reflect the centrality of families.
Headquartered at 11 East Adams Street, Suite 240, Chicago, IL 60603. Phone: (312) 663-0305.
CEO listed as Susana Marotta (per ContactOut, as of early 2026).
Services are always FREE to participants.

Four core pillars: (1) Parenting — parent education, support for parenting teens; (2) Early Childhood Education — Early Head Start, Head Start; (3) Education Supports — academic support for school-age youth; (4) Workforce Development — career coaching, subsidized employment (e.g., Lake County Youth Employment Program, $16.50/hr for ages 16–24).
Also runs Community Services programs for needs outside the core cycle (e.g., stability, crisis support).
Has a Cycle Breakers/SCAN (Service Coordination and Navigation) program in the Roseland neighborhood (Chicago's South Side), focused on mentoring and restorative justice for youth ages 14–18.
Serves communities throughout Illinois: city of Chicago, suburban Cook County, Lake County, and Will County.
Offers both in-person and online classes/programs.
Tagline/motto: 'Strive, Grow & Achieve through SGA's Cycle of Opportunity®.'
Has filed Form 990 with ProPublica (most recently filed Jan. 23, 2025), indicating it spends $750,000+ in federal grant money.
Partner of United Way of Metro Chicago.
News/reviews note some internal management/turnover challenges but broadly positive mission alignment among staff.

The email prefix 'jreyes2' suggests there may be more than one 'J. Reyes' at the organization (the '2' suffix is common when a second person shares the same first-initial/last-name combination). No profile on SGA's leadership/board page, LinkedIn, or any other indexed source was found for this individual. CEO is listed as Susana Marotta. No founder or prominent figure named Joshua Reyes is associated with this organization in any source found.
